decks.name: English Riddles — Intermédiaire
decks.description: 15 énigmes : du vocabulaire anglais du quotidien, avec des indices un peu plus subtils.

clues: People forget me everywhere.
I open and close.
You need me on a grey day.
I keep you dry.
You hold me over your head when it rains.
I am a very quiet place.
You must whisper inside me.
You can borrow things from me for free.
I am full of shelves.
I am a building full of books.
You take me on holiday.
You pack me before a trip.
Airlines sometimes lose me.
I roll on little wheels through airports.
I am the bags and suitcases you travel with.
I am a way of speaking.
You do it in a library or at the cinema.
You do it when you tell a secret.
I am the opposite of shouting.
I mean to speak very, very quietly.
You see this person almost every day.
You might share a wall or a fence with them.
A good one lends you sugar or waters your plants.
They live next door.
This person lives in the house or flat beside yours.
I connect two places.
Thanks to me, you cross without getting wet.
Cars, trains or people can use me.
I go over rivers and valleys.
A famous London one "is falling down", says the song.
You probably have me in your pocket or bag right now.
Losing me is a nightmare.
I am often made of leather.
I hold your cards and your ID.
You keep your money in me.
I am soft and warm.
You use me mostly at night.
Small children sometimes drag me everywhere.
You hide under me during scary films.
You put me on your bed to stay warm.
You hear me during a storm.
I can wake you up at night.
Dogs are often afraid of me.
You see my partner before you hear me.
I am the loud noise that follows lightning.
I am a tool.
In English, my name is always plural.
Be careful, I am sharp.
In a famous hand game, I beat paper but lose to rock.
You use me to cut paper, hair or fabric.
I organize your life.
Trains and buses are supposed to follow me.
When you are busy, people say I am "full".
I tell you what happens, and when.
I am the plan of your day — your timetable.
You get me after paying.
Many people throw me away immediately.
You need me to return something to a shop.
The cashier prints me.
I am the small piece of paper that proves you bought something.
You find me in almost every home.
You open me in the morning and close me at night.
At the theatre, when I fall, the show is over.
I hang in front of your window.
I am the fabric that blocks the light from outside.
I happen mostly at the end of summer.
Farmers wait for me all year.
Big machines called combines help with me.
I am the moment when crops are collected.
I mean gathering wheat, corn or grapes from the fields.
I appear after the rain.
Children love to jump in me.
I disappear when the sun comes back.
Cars splash you when they drive through me.
I am a small pool of water on the ground.
